description: It consists of the white background. In the centre is placed a red lion, that stands on its back legs, have raised its front paws, and put its tongue out. Around it are placed in a circle nine gentiana flowers that have five intense blue petals and white (silver) round centres.

From historical and heraldical points of views, it was better to stick to the old Brabant tradition of chequered flags. This tradition traces back at least to the insurrection against the absolutism of Filip VI. The association of red, yellow, blue and white from the main colours of Antwerp (red-white), Mechelen (yellow-red) and Turnhout (white-blue) can easily be compared to the historical models.
Three stripes with proportions 5:6:5, the upper and lower stripes green, the middle stripe alternate white and green with proportions 1:1:1:1:4:1:1, in the fly a black lion with red nails and tongue.

The flag is a banner of the provincial arms; in practice, the lion is shown dark yellow/orange. The arms o are "Sable a lion or armed and langued gules an escutcheon gules a fess argent, the shield surmuonted by a Ducal coronet and supported by two lions or armed and langued gules on a wrought iron or". The escutcheoh is the coat of arms of Leuven, the capital of the province, but also the capital of the old Duchy of Lotharingia.

The provincial flag of Limburg is white with a red lion with yellow tongue and claws. The lion bears a Ducal coronet and an escutcheon made of ten yellow and red horizontal stripes. It is a banner of the provincial arms.
Azure and white horizontal stripes with the crowned red lion[2]

description:
A vertical tricolour of red (for the Bosnian Croats), white, and green (for the Bosniaks), with a coat of arms on the wide central band on which the green arms and golden fleur-de-lys represents the Bosniaks, and the checked shield the Bosnian Croats.
The Constitutional Court of Bosnia and Herzegovina voted against the use of the current flag of the Federation declaring it unconstitutional.[3] On 31 March 2007, the Constitutional Court placed its decision into the "Official Gazette of Bosnia and Herzegovina" officially removing the flag and coat of arms of Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina.[4]

name: Brittany
description: The flag, called Gwen ha du (White and black) was created in 1923 by Morvan Marchal (1900–1963, a member of various political and cultural organisations). He used as his inspiration the old Breton flag (a centred black cross on a white background), called Kroaz Du (Black cross), and the flags of the United States and Greece as these two countries were seen at that time as the respective symbols of liberty and democracy. The nine horizontal stripes represent the traditional dioceses of Brittany into which the duchy was divided historically. The five black stripes represent the French or Gallo speaking dioceses of Dol, Nantes, Rennes, Saint-Malo and Saint-Brieuc—while the four white stripes represent the Breton speaking dioceses of Trégor, Léon, Cornouaille and Vannes. The ermine canton recalls the ducal arms of Brittany.

description: The Flag of Corsica was adopted by General of the Nation Pasquale di Paoli in 1755 and was based on a traditional flag used previously. It portrays a Moor's Head in black wearing a white bandana above his eyes on a white background. Previously, the bandana covered his eyes.

This symbol, three gold Fleur-de-lis on a solid-blue background, is a banner of the former royal coat of arms of France, the king historically being the direct lord of Île de France (as the core of his royal demesne).

name: Saint Olav flag, using a Nordic cross in reference to the Nordic past of Normandy, created by Jean Adigard des Gautries in 1937.

The red lion has been used in the area since the eleventh century, appearing in the coats of arms of Poitiers and several other settlements in the region. The waves represent the name "Aquitaine", which stems from the Latin Aquitaina (land of water).

name: Macedonia (Greece)

name: Spetses
description: The flag of Spetses, adopted in early 1821 during the Greek Revolution, features an azure field surrounded by a thick red border, defaced with a Cross on an overturned Crescent (symbolizing the Christian Greek victory over the Muslim Ottoman Empire), a spear (symbolizing the armed struggle for freedom), an anchor (symbolizing the maritime tradition and merchant wealth of the island as well as most of Greece), a snake around the anchor (symbolizing the Goddess Athena, wisdom and the island's ancient Greek heritage), a dove (symbolizing peace, justice and prosperity) and the words «ΕΛΕΥΘΕΡΙΑ Ή ΘΑΝΑΤΟΣ», meaning "FREEDOM OR DEATH", Greece's National Motto.

name: Hydra
description: The flag of Hydra, adopted in early 1821 during the Greek Revolution, its design and symbolism are almost identical to those of the Flag of Spetses (see above), though the colours are slightly different. The Symbols are also almost identical, though instead of a spear there is a flagpole with a flag and some of the symbols are grey instead of red.

name: Corfu
description: The flag of the island of Corfu, adopted in 1864 after the annexation of the United States of the Ionian Islands by the Kingdom of Greece as a gift to George I of Greece by the United Kingdom, features a blue field surrounded by a golden border and a thicker dark red one. It is defaced with an Ancient Greek Galley which is surrounded by a circle and the words «ΔΗΜΟΣ ΚΕΡΚΥΡΑΙΩΝ» meaning "MUNICIPALITY/PEOPLE OF THE CORFIOTS".

name: Zakynthos
description: The flag of the island of Zakynthos, adopted in 1864 after the annexation of the United States of the Ionian Islands by the Kingdom of Greece as a gift to George I of Greece by the United Kingdom, features a Green field with a golden depiction of Zakynthos (person), the figure from Ancient Greek Mythology after whom the island was named. Near the top left side of Zakynthos the name of the island in Greek («ΖΑΚΥΝΘΟΣ») is written while under the depiction is the island's motto, «ΘΕΛΕΙ ΑΡΕΤΗ ΚΑΙ ΤΟΛΜΗ Η ΕΛΕΥΘΕΡΙΑ» ("FREEDOM DEMANDS VALOUR AND BRAVERY") by the Zakynthian poet Andreas Kalvos.

name: Psara
description: The flag of the island of Psara, adopted in 1821 during the Greek Revolution, is almost identical in style and design to the flags of Hydra, Spetses and other islands (see above) with some differences. The field is white and the surrounding border is red while all the symbols it is defaced with are all red. The symbols are also almost identical though instead of a dove there is an eagle, probably a reference to the Eagle of Zeus, a symbol of power and authority. The flag features the words «ΕΛΕΥΘΕΡΙΑ Ή ΘΑΝΑΤΟΣ» ("FREEDOM OR DEATH", Greece's National Motto) and «ΨΑΡΑ» ("PSARA", the island's name in the Greek language).

name: Mani Peninsula
description: The flag of the island of the Mani Peninsula, adopted in 1821 during the Greek Revolution, features a white field with a blue Greek cross and the phrases «ΝΙΚΗ Ή ΘΑΝΑΤΟΣ» ("VICTORY OR DEATH", a traditional Greek battle cry) and «ΤΑΝ Ή ΕΠΙ ΤΑΣ» (a Laconic phrase said by Spartan mothers to their sons when they left for war; lit. "IT OR ON IT" meaning that if the Spartan is to return alive he should return with his shield [so as to have not dropped it and fled the battle] or be returned on it by his comrades, dead).

description: A horizontal bicolor of white and green with the county's coat of arms on the hoist side.

name: Zala County
description: A horizontal bicolor of blue and white with the county's coat of arms and the inscription "ZALA MEGYE" (Zala County) in the center.

name: Ireland
description: Green, white and orange vertical

name: Connacht
description: The flag of Connacht is a heraldic banner of the arms of Connacht, a dimidiated (divided in half from top to bottom) eagle and armed hand.

name: Leinster
description: The flag of the Irish province of Leinster is a banner with the provincial coat of arms: a gold Irish harp with silver strings on a green field (blazon: vert a harp or stringed argent). These arms are similar to the arms of Ireland, which have the same device on a field of blue rather than green

name: Munster
description: The flag of Munster consists of three gold crowns on a blue field. The crowns were the arms of Ireland before being superseded by the golden harp in the 16th century. The meaning of the crowns on the flag is not certain, but one possibility is that they may represent three of the medieval Hiberno-Norman lordships in Munster; the O’Briens (Thomond), the Butlers (Ormond) and the Fitzgeralds (Desmond).

name: Ulster
description: Ulster is one of the four traditional provinces of Ireland. Only three of the nine counties of Ulster are part of the Republic of Ireland, the other six making up Northern Ireland, part of the United Kingdom. However, the flag is still used to represent Ulster as a whole. The Red Hand of Ulster is a symbol that is either derived from the O'Neill dynasty, then the most prominent Irish clan in Ulster, or the Dextra Dei of early Christian iconography. The gold background featuring a red cross comes from the coat of arms of the Burkes, a Hiberno-Norman noble family.

name: Italy
description: Green, white and red vertical

name: Abruzzo
description: A burgundy field with the coat of arms of Abruzzo in the centre

name: the Aosta Valley
description: The flag of the Aosta Valley consists of two vertical stripes (the left black the right red), sometimes with the arms of the Aosta Valley in the centre.

name: Apulia
description: A white field with the words Regione Puglia ("Apulia Region") in gold letters at the top center, with the coat of arms of Apulia below; a green stripe towards the hoist-side, and a red stripe towards the fly-side.

name: Basilicata
description: A field of azure with the coat of arms of Basilicata in the centre

name: Calabria
description: A blue field with the coat of arms of Calabria in the centre, and the words "Regione Calabria" above and below

name: Campania
description:
The Campania Region has taken as its emblem the one given the Maritime Republic of Amalfi in its infancy. Said emblem consists of a red stripe on a white field.
Regional Law n. 1 of 21 July 1971

name: Emilia Romagna
description: The emblem of the region superimposed upon a field of white, with a red bar and the words "Regione Emilia-Romagna" below

name: Friuli-Venezia Giulia
description: A golden eagle facing to its right standing on white fortifications on a blue background.

name: Lazio
description: The coat of arms of Lazio surrounded by laurel and olive branches, surmounted by a golden crown on a sky-blue field with the words "Regione Lazio" in gold

name: Lombardy
description: A green field with the Rosa Camuna (also called curvilinear cross) in white, rotated 22.5° in the centre

name: Liguria
description: A vertical tricolour of green, red and blue; charged with the coat of arms of Liguria.

name: Marche
description: A white field with the regional emblem of Marche.

name: Molise
description: A field of blue with the coat of arms in the centre, and "Regione Molise" in gold below

name: Piedmont
description: Banner of arms of the Prince of Piedmont with a heraldic label

name: Sardinia
description: Four moors head in St. George's cross

name: Sicily
description:
The current flag was adopted on 28 July 1990 under regional law N. 2, Art. 12. The law was later updated in 1998. It was not until 1 January 2000, under regional law N. 1, that the flag was adopted as the official symbol of Sicily, including legislation mandating public display of the flag at all Sicilian public buildings. The flag, officially used by the ancient Kingdom of Sicily, has existed in various forms since 1282.
The flag is rectangular in form and is characterised by the presence of the Trinacria (triskelion) in the centre. It features a softened image of the winged-head of Medusa and three ears of wheat (replacing snakeheads) representing the island's fertility. The three bent legs represent the island's three capes or points, while in mythology it is said to represent good luck and prosperity. The background of the flag is divided by a diagonal from left to right, and is coloured gold (lower left) and red-orange (upper right). The colours represent the cities of Palermo and Corleone, respectively, the first two to found a confederation against the Angevin rule.

name: Trentino-Alto Adige-Sudtirol
description: A horizontal bicolour of white and blue, with the coat of arms of Trentino-South Tyrol superimposed on top

name: Tuscany
description: A white field with a silver Pegasus rampant in the center between two red horizontal bands.

name: Umbria
description: green with the emblem

name: Veneto
description: The arms of Regione del Veneto on a Pompeian red background; on the fly edge, seven tails bearing the coat of arms of the seven province capitals of Veneto.

name: Lithuania
description: Yellow, red and orange horizontal

name: Liechtenstein
description: Blue and red with crown

name: Luxembourg
description: Red, white and blue horizontal

name: Malta
description: White and red vertical

name: Gozo
description: Symbolises the islands nickname 'The Island of the Three Hills', and also the fact that it is surrounded by sea.

name: Netherlands
description: Red, white and blue horizontal

name: Drenthe
description: White and red are the colours of a former leader, the Bishop of Utrecht. The black castle and red stars are a memory of Coevorden standing up against the Bisshop.

name: Flevoland
description: The flag recalls how the new province was reclaimed from the IJsselmeer. The central yellow stripe, wavy then straight, symbolises the transformation of the sea into land. Its colour symbolises rapeseed, planted to stabilise the land. The blue represents water, the green the land. The white fleur-de-lys (lily) is a pun. It commemorates Cornelis Lely, designer of the original polders, essential to the province.

name: Friesland
description: The flag is based on the kings of Frysia in 15th century. The colours are the same as those of the national flag of the Netherlands. The stripes and leaves of the yellow water-lily, represent the 7 districts of Friesland.

name: Gelderland
description: The colours are taken from the coat of arms, in which the lions from former duchesses are combined.

name: Groningen
description: The flag combines the colours of the city of Groningen with red and blue in the flag of the area around Groningen.

name: Limburg
description: The red lion comes from the coat of arms of the Duchy of Limburg. The white and yellow are taken from the local coat of arms, while the narrow blue stripe symbolises the Meuse, which flows through the province.

name: North Brabant
description: The North Brabantian flag dates from the Middle Ages and has the highest seniority among all Dutch province flags. The colours gules and argent have been used in Brabant standards, flags and pennants since the proclamation of the County of Louvain (942 CE) during the Lotharingian period. Later, the Duchy of Brabant continued using these colours. During the Middle Ages and the centuries after, the red and white would often be used. At the end of the 18th century, the flag fell into disuse. Since 1959, the red and white checkered flag has become the official flag of the province of North Brabant.

name: North Holland
description: The colours of the flag are similar to the colours of the coat of arms, which is a combination of the coat of arms from Holland and West Friesland.

name: Overijssel
description: The yellow and red stripes are a reference to the former association of this province with Holland. The blue source in the middle stands for the river IJssel, which gave the province its name.

name: South Holland
description: The flag is a banner of the coat of arms of Holland.

name: Utrecht
description: From 1528, the bishop of Utrecht used a red flag with a white cross. This flag is still seen in the upper canton.

name: Zeeland
description: In the centre of the flag, the coat of arms of Zeeland is depicted. The wavy blue lines represent the waves and the constant struggle against the sea. The lion is a symbol for the winner of this battle between man and sea.

name: Poland
description: White and red horizontal

name: Greater Poland Voivodeship
description: Trapezoid divided into red stripe on the right, and white stripe on the left, with a white eagle with yellow (golden) beak, legs, stripes on its wings, and a ring on its tail, featured in the middle of the red stripe

name: Holy Cross Voivodeship
description: Rectangle divided into four parts. Its left part features a yellow vertical stripe, which wight equals to 1:4 of the wight of the flag. Its right part is divided into three horizontal stripes, that are, from top to bottom: blue, white, and red. In the middle of the white stripe is placed the coat of arms of the voivodeship.

name: Kuyavian-Pomeranian Voivodeship
description: Three horizontal stripes of red, white, and black colour, with the middle stripe being twice the size of the other ones

name: Lesser Poland Voivodeship
description: Three horizontal stripes of white, yellow, and red, with the top and bottom stripes being twice the size of the middle one

name: Lower Silesia Voivodeship
description: Yellow (golden) rectangle with the left-faced black eagle, with a white (silver) crescent-shaped przepaska put across its wings, with a white (silver) cross pattée on top of it, in its middle.

name: Lublin Voivodeship
description: Three horizontal stripes: white (silver), red, and yellow (golden), with the coat of arms of the voivodeship, in form of the white (silver) male cervus mid-jump, with a yellow (golden) crown on its neck, placed on the red escutcheon, placed in the centre of the flag

name: Lubusz Voivodeship
description: Four stripes, which are, from top to bottom, yellow (golden), white, (silver), red, and green, with top and bottom stripes being twice the size of the 2 middle stripes

name: Łódź Voivodeship
description: Rectangle divided into 5 vertical stripes, that are altering between red and yellow

name: Mazovia Voivodeship
description: Red rectangle with white eagle with yellow beak and legs, on the left side

name: Opole Voivodeship
description: Two horizontal stripes, with yellow on the top, and blue at the bottom, of which, the top stripe is twice the size of the bottom one

name: Podlachia Voivodeship
description: Four horizontal stripes, that are, from the top to bottom: white, red, yellow, and blue

name: Pomerania Voivodeship
description: Yellow rectangle with a left-faced black griffin placed in the centre

name: Silesia Voivodeship
description: Three horizontal stripes, that are from top to bottom: blue, yellow, and blue, with blue stripes being twice the size of the yellow middle stripe

name: Subcarpathian Voivodeship
description: Rectangle divided verticaly in 3 stripes with smaller white stripes on the sides, and bigger white stripe in the middle, with the coat of arms of the Subcarpathian Voivodeship in centre.

name: Warmia-Masuria Voivodeship
description: Red flag with a white boundary on its top, bottom, and right sides, and with the head of a white (silver) eagle, with yellow (golden) beak, and an eye, wearing a yellow (golden) crown, located on its left side

name: West Pomerania Voivodeship
description: Rectangle divided vertically in 3 stripes with white, red, and white colour, and with the coat of arms of the West Pomeranian Voivodeship in the middle

name: Portugal
description: Green and red with sphere

name: the Azores
description: The flag of Azores has the colours of the flag of Portugal until 1910. It also has 9 stars representing the islands of the archipelago, the Portuguese shield and a Goshawk (in Portuguese Açor) that gives the name to the archipelago. It is an adaption of the first autonomy flag used during the autonomic movement in 1893, itself adapted of the then monarchist flag.

name: Madeira
description: The design consists of a blue-gold-blue vertical triband with a red-bordered white Cross of Christ in the centre.

name: Romania
description: Blue, yellow and red vertical

name: Arad (municipality)
description: A bright yellow flag with a blue horizontal wavy strip, symbolizing the Mureș river and the city coat of arms centered.

name: Russia
description: White, blue and red horizontal

name: Adygea
description: Twelve golden stars resembling a bow charged with three golden arrows on a dark green background

name: Arkhangelsk Oblast
description: A light blue saltire on a field of white with the coat of arms of the Arkhangelsk Oblast in the center

name: Astrakhan Oblast
description: A crown and scimitar charge on a teal blue field.

name: Bashkortostan
description: A horizontal tricolor of teal blue, white and green with a yellow circle bearing the kurai flower.

name: Belgorod Oblast
description: A blue cross with four cantons (white, green, black and red) and the coat of arms on the white canton.

name: Bryansk Oblast
description: A burgundy field defaced with the coat of arms of the Bryansk Oblast in the center

name: Chechnya
description: A horizontal tricolor of green (representing Islam), white (representing peace or the Caucasus Mountains) and red (representing bloodshed). The national ornament is at the white band at the hoist.

name: Chuvashia
description: A yellow flag with a stylized red tree of life charged on the flag, a symbol of rebirth, with the three suns, a traditional emblem popular in Chuvash art.

name: Crimea[note 1]
description: A horizontal tricolor of a thin blue stripe, a large white stripe, and thin red stripe.

name: Dagestan
description: A horizontal tricolor of green, blue, and red.

name: Ingushetia
description: A red triskelion sign on a white background, with narrow green horizontal stripes above and below

name: Kabardino-Balkaria
description: A tricolor of light blue, white, and green with a Mount Elbrus charge in the center.

name: Kaliningrad Oblast
description: Divided into three horizontal stripes. The upper stripe is red, a thin (1/3 of the upper strip) yellow stripe in the middle and a dark blue stripe of the same size as the red bar. In the canton is a silver-and-black stylized medieval castle with open gates and the monogram of Empress Elizabeth Petrovna (under which reign parts of the region were shortly under Russian control during the Seven Years' War).

name: Kalmykia
description: On the flag of Kalmykia, the yellow stands for the sun, the people and the religious faith of the nation. The blue represents the sky, eternity, and steadiness. The lotus is a symbol of purity, spiritual rebirth and happiness. Its five upper petals represent the continents and the lower four stand for the quarters of the globe. Together, they symbolize the will of the Kalmyks to live in friendship and to cooperate with all the nations of the world.

name: Karachay-Cherkessia
description: A horizontal tricolour of light blue, green and red, with a circle containing a rising sun from behind a mountain, centered on the green band

name: Karelia
description: The national flag of the Republic of Karelia is a rectangle with equal horizontal stripes: the upper stripe is red, the middle one is blue and the lower is green.

name: the Komi Republic
description: The flag is a horizontal tricolour composed of three bars of, from top to bottom, medium blue, green, and white. Together, they represent Komi's natural wealth. The blue represents the splendour and spaciousness of the northern sky. The green represents nature, its bounty, and the taiga. The white represents the color of snow, the purity of nature in the north, simplicity, and austerity, as well as Komi being a country in the north. According to a different interpretation, the white represents the equality and unity of the people and cultures living in Komi.

name: Krasnodar Krai
description: Horizontal tricolour of blue, pink and green charged with the golden coat of arms

name: the Mari El Republic
description: A white field with a ornament pattern on the hoist and the Mari coat of arms offset to the right.

name: Mordovia
description: A horizontal tricolor of red, white and blue with an eight-pointed red cross.

name: Moscow Municipality
description: Dark red background with its coat of arms on it

name: North Ossetia–Alania
description: A tri-horizontal bar flag with the colors white, red, and yellow

name: Saint Petersburg
description: A red field charged in the centre with the arms of the city, which consists of two silver anchors (a fluked anchor, and a grapnel anchor), and a gold scepter.

name: Sevastopol[note 2]
description: Seal of the city on a red field.

name: Tatarstan
description: Red and green divided by a white fimbriation.

name: the Udmurt Republic
description: The cross/star symbol represents the solar sign, a protective symbol that according to folklore protects man from misfortunes.

name: Volgograd Oblast
description: Red field with two vertical stripes at the hoist, charged with the statue The Motherland Calls.

name: Serbia
description: Red, white and blue with eagle

name: Vojvodina
description: Horizontal tricolour of red, blue and white with larger blue portion and three yellow stars.

name: Traditional flag of Vojvodina
description: Horizontal tricolour of red, blue and white with a coat of arms.

name: Slovakia
description: White, blue and red with cross

name: Spain
description: Red-yellow-red horizontal

name: Andalusia
description: The flag of Andalusia consists of a horizontal tricolour (green-white-green) with the Andalusian arms in the centre.

name: Aragon
description: Nine stripes, alternating red and yellow, starting with yellow and the coat of arms of Aragon on the left half of the flag.

name: Asturias
description: The flag of Asturias is light blue with the Victory Cross slightly left of the centre.

name: the Balearic Islands
description: The flag of the Balearic Islands, made up of distinctive, historically legitimised symbols, will consist of four horizontal red bars over a yellow background, having an upper-left quarter with a purple background behind a centred white castle with five turrets.

name: the Basque Country
description: A red field with the white central cross that extends to the edges of the flag superimposed on the green diagonal cross that extends to the corners of the flag.

name: Cantabria
description: Two horizontal bands of same width, white on top, red on the bottom

name: the Canary Islands
description: The flag of the Autonomous Community of the Canary Islands is a vertical tricolour of three equal bands of white, blue, and yellow. The state flag includes the Coat of arms of the Canary Islands in the central band; the civil flag omits this. The designs were made official by the Statute of Autonomy of the Canarian Autonomous Community (Organic Law 10/82) on 16 August 1982.

name: Castilla–La Mancha
description: The flag of the region is made up of a rectangle divided vertically into two equal squares: the first, next to the mast, of crimson red with a castle of gold mauled of saber and ringed of blue and the second, white.

name: Castile and León
description: It is formed by the combination of the historical flags of two of the oldest kingdom is the Iberian peninsula in the Middle Ages, the Kingdom of León (purple lion on silver in reference to the meaning of "león") and the Kingdom of Castile (golden castle on red in reference to the many castles that were erected all over the country). The joint historical flag dates back to mid 13th century when Ferdinand III, "the Saint", unified the two kingdoms in 1230, inaugurating the Kingdom of Castile and León, later named Crown of Castile.

name: Catalonia
description: The flag of Catalonia consists four red bars on top of a yellow background. One popular (and unproven) legend mentions this flag originating with the streaking of 4 blood-stained fingers across a plain shield. The flag derives from the royal coat of arms of the historical royal family of the Crown of Aragon, House of Barcelona.

name: Extremadura
description: Three horizontal stripes of green, white, and black, with a coat of arms in the center toward the hoist.

name: Galicia
description: The flag of Galicia appeared for the first time in the 19th century, probably based on the colours of the ancient medieval flags of the Kingdom of Galicia. Originally, the flag was a blue St Andrew's Cross over a white field—St Andrew is one of the most popular saints in Galicia. The coat of arms of Galicia was the former flag of the Kingdom of Galicia. Colors blue, white and gold were always related with Galicia. The chalice and the golden crosses on blue background have been its symbol since medieval times (13th century). For some time it was thought that it was based on the flag of the maritime province of Corunna, but today it is known that the design is earlier.

name: La Rioja
description: Four horizontal bands of equal size, with the colors of red, white, green, and yellow, with a coat of arms in the center of the flag.

name: the Community of Madrid
description: The seven stars represent the stars of the constellation Ursa Minor. They're five-pointed because they represent the five Spanish provinces which surround the Community of Madrid.

name: the Region of Murcia
description: The flag of the Region of Murcia is rectangular and consists of four castles with battlements or, in the upper left corner, arranged in rows of two, and seven royal crowns in the lower right corner, arranged in four rows, with a pattern of one, three, two, and one, respectively; against a crimson or carmine red background

name: Navarre
description: The flag of Navarre is red-colored, with a shield in the center.

name: the Valencian Community
description: The traditional Senyera, composed of four red bars on a yellow background, crowned with a blue strip party per pale next to the hoist.

name: Sweden
description: Blue with yellow cross

name: Skåne County (Scania)
description: Also a similar flag exists with blue background for Scania region

name: The county of Jämtland
description: Jamtland (that belonged to Norway until 1645) has its own movement for the Republic of Jamtland – not a serious attempt for independence but more a touch of humor and local culture. The flag however is used in the area.

name: Jämtland County
description: Compare unofficial above. Jämtland modern county includes the two traditional provinces Jämtland and Härjedalen.

name: The traditional province of Småland
description: Småland is a relatively large traditional province in the south of Sweden that today is divided in 3 administrative counties and also with 2 smaller areas outside these 3 counties.

name: Kronoberg County
description: Part of historical province of Småland (see above)

name: Jönköping County
description: Part of historical province of Småland (see above)

name: Kalmar County
description: Part of historical province of Småland (see above) and the island province Öland (see below).

name: The historical province and island Öland outside Småland in the Baltic Sea
description: Öland (Swedish means "The Island Land") has a long history and different nature compared to the main land of Sweden. Öland is the second largest island in Sweden.

name: The traditional province Östergötland
description: The blue cross relates to the two main channels and the yellow to agricultural flat areas. Not much in use.

name: Östergötland County
description: Compare unofficial above.

name: Västra Götaland County
description: Flag includes arms/flags from the 4 traditional provinces (Västergötland, Bohuslän, Dalsland and Älvsborg) that are included in this relatively newly formed regional county.

name: The traditional province Västergötland
description: Not much in use. Also compare the previous flag above that covers the same area and more.

name: Blekinge County
description: The coat of arms for the traditional province of Blekinge and the administrative Blekinge county. Not much in use.

name: Halland County
description: There is a geographical difference between the administrative Halland county and the traditional Halland province.

name: Värmland County
description: There is a geographical difference between the administrative Värmland county and the traditional Värmland province.

name: Örebro County
description: The Örebro county includes the traditional province Närke and parts of traditional provinces of Värmland and Västmanland. The flag includes arms from these 3 counties.

name: Södermanland County
description: The administrative Södermanland County includes parts (southern part) of traditional Södermanland County

name: Uppsala County
description: The administrative Uppsala County includes parts (northern part) of traditional Uppland Province

name: Stockholm County
description: The administrative Stockholm County includes parts (southern part) of traditional Uppland County and (northern part) of traditional Södermanland province. the flag includes arms from these both counties and also the arms of Stockholm city.

name: Gotland County
description: The administrative Gotland County consists of one main and a number of smaller islands in the Baltic sea. This is the only county in Sweden that only has one municipality.

name: Västmanland County
description: The administrative Västmanland County includes parts of traditional Västmanland province

name: Dalarna County
description: This county was earlier known as Kopparbergs county and even earlier as Dalarna Province

name: Gävleborg County
description: Gävleborg administrative County encompasses the traditional provinces of Gästrikland and Hälsingland.

name: Västernorrland County
description: Västernorrland administrative county covers approximately the traditional province of Ångermanland and the traditional province of Medelpad.

name: Västerbotten County
description: Västerbotten administrative county covers the traditional province of Västerbotten and parts of the traditional province Swedish Lapland and traditional province of Ångermanland.

name: Norrbotten County
description: Norrbotten County includes the traditional province of Norrbotten and about two-thirds of the traditional province of Swedish Lapland.

name: Coastal area of Roslagen
description: Roslagen is the name of the coastal areas of Uppland province in Sweden, which also constitutes the northern part of the Stockholm archipelago. Norrtälje is sometimes named the capital city of Roslagen.

name: Switzerland
description: Red with white cross

name: Bern
description: A red field a yellow diagonal band charged with a black bear with a red tongue, claws walking upwards toward the hoist.

name: Geneva
description: The flag of Geneva is the historical flag of the city of Geneva, showing the Imperial Eagle and a Key of St. Peter (symbolizing the status of Geneva as Reichsstadt and as episcopal seat, respectively), in use since the 15th century.

name: Glarus
description: Gules, a pilgrim walking towards the dexter argent, habited sable, holding in his dexter hand a staff, in his sinister hand a bible, and above his head a halo.

name: St. Gallen
description: A white upright fasces with the axe blade facing the hoist on green field.

name: Schwyz
description: A white banner with a cross on the top-left

name: Uri
description: A bull's head seen face on, with a red tongue and a red nose ring, on a yellow field.

name: Ukraine
description: Blue and yellow horizontal

name: Cherkasy Oblast
description: The larger half of it has a dark blue color, which on the center features the oblast's coat of arms.

name: Chernihiv Oblast
description: The flag is a rectangular green panel with an aspect ratio of 2:3, the middle of which is a white horizontal stripe (width 1/5 of the flag's width), and in the upper pole of the white square shows the emblem area.

name: Chernivtsi Oblast
description: On the flag, there is a rectangular panel with a ratio of 2:3. From the top and bottom edges are blue-and-yellow stripes. The width of the blue strip equals 1/10 of a flag's total width, and the yellow stripes width equals 1/30 of the flag's width. At the center of the flag is a white falcon on a green background. The falcons height equals 1/2 of the height of the flag.

name: Crimea[a]
description: A horizontal tricolor of a thin blue stripe, a large white stripe, and thin red stripe. Controlled by Russia but recognised as part of Ukraine by most of the international community.

name: Donetsk Oblast
description: A rising gold sun with 12 sunbeams and five gold ovals one under another

name: Dnipropetrovsk Oblast
description: The flag is a rectangle divided by a diagonal stripe into a blue part (upper right) and a white part (lower left). The images on the flag reproduce the graphic elements of the coat of arms of the Dnipropetrovsk region, they are: nine stars on the blue part and a Cossack with musket on the left part. The flag's dividing line consists of repeating wave patterns: yellow at the top and blue at the bottom.

name: Ivano-Frankivsk Oblast
description: White field with a black jackdaw. Both the vertical edges are decorated by two different narrow bicolors.

name: Kharkiv Oblast
description: Coat of arms of Kharkiv on Crimson field

name: Kherson Oblast
description: A rectangular panel with an aspect ratio of 2:3 is divided by three horizontal stripes - blue, white and blue - in a ratio of 1:2:1. On the white stripe near the pole is the coat of arms of the Oblast.

name: Kirovohrad Oblast
description: It is divided into two vertical stripes: raspberry on the left and yellow on the right. On the raspberry stripe is the coat of arms: an image of a yellow steppe eagle, which is based on a golden statuette found in the Scythian Melgunov Kurgan.

name: Kyiv
description: An azure field with a yellow border with Archangel Michael centered on the flag.

name: Luhansk Oblast
description: Oblast coat of arms in top left corner, surrounded by seventeen yellow and fourteen white stars

name: Lviv Oblast
description: Yellow lion on a blue field

name: Poltava Oblast
description: Yellow Cossack cross on a blue field

name: Sevastopol[m]
description: Seal of the city. Controlled by Russia but recognised as part of Ukraine by most of the international community.

name: Sumy Oblast
description: Oblast coat of arms on blue field

name: Ternopil Oblast
description: A rectangular blue banner with an aspect ratio of 2:3, a yellow sword and a key placed in the middle, and three white towers with yellow accents above them.

name: Vinnytsia Oblast
description: A blue rectangular banner with an aspect ratio of 2:3. In the center of the flag are the coats of arms of Podolia, the golden sun, and the Bratslav region (Eastern Podolia), a silver cross with a blue shield with a silver crescent. Two red stripes are placed horizontally in the upper and lower parts at a distance of 1/10 from the edge of the canvas and 1/10 of the width.

name: Volyn Oblast
description: In the center on a red (color of ripe cherry) background is an isosceles cross of white (silver) color, touching the ends of the edges of the flag. In the upper left corner is one of the oldest versions of the historical crosses of Volhynia of the 15th to the 18th century centuries (so-called Cross pattée).

name: United Kingdom

The white layout represents Matale District and Nuwaraeiya District which represents the Upcountry is symbolised by the white lotus.  The Sun and Moon are for Eternity.

name: Southern Province
description:
A picture of a lion flag can be seen in frescos in historical Dambulla cave temple.  This historical flag made of carving a picture of a running lion with a small sword in its forepaw is considered to be the Ruhuna flag.
Furthermore, King Dutugamunu left the Magam Kingdom with the lion flag ahead for the battle to unite the country.  The flag is a picture of a yellow lion on a red blood colour background.  Accordingly, it was decided to use for the Southern Provincial flag the picture of the lion running with a small sword (iluk koolaya) in its forepaw of the Ruhuna flag and the red colour and yellow colour of the flag taken on the war front by King Dutugamunu.
It was decided to use the same for the Southern Province flag mixture of colours and the standard to represent all communities in Galle, Matara, and Hambantota and four bo leaves (Metta, Karuna, Muditha, Upeksha) of the national flag.
It was agreed that the forms of the sun and moon of the flag of Southern Province flag should be in the same forms as the sun and moon of the flag of Devinuwara Devalaya and that the same form that of the flag of Hambantota District.  Accordingly, it was expected to keep the integration of Galle, matara and Hambantota.  Therefore, the Southern Province flag can be considered a combination of several traditional flags.
The small sword (iluk Koolaya) is the symbol of control, but it does not reflect terror or suppression.  The small sword represents Justice and fairness.  Running lion represents velocity fearlessness and pride.
The sun and moon stand for stability prosperity and augustness.  It expresses the traditional saying "Until the sun and moon exists". The sun and moon of the flag are considered to be symbols of victory. The combination of colours around the flag depicts the existence of Southern Sri Lanka, victorious war history, religion and patriotism, mutual cooperation and harmony

name: Uva Province
description:
In the days of the Sinhala kings, Sri Lanka had been divided into twelve provinces and ruled and each of these twelve provinces had been allotted a flag. Accordingly, in order to bring about qualities such as pleasantness, innocence, greatness and royalty, a flag with a picture of swan had been allotted to the Uva Province.
The flag that was gifted to Uva Province by the King Sri  Wickrama Rajasinghe who ruled the kingdom of Kandy during the period 1798–1815 can be seen even today at the National Museum ( Courtesy:  Book entitled “Uva Ithihasaya” by Panditha Naulle Dhammananda Thero )

description: A red field with the white-edged green Nordic cross that extends to the edges; the vertical part of the cross is shifted to the hoist side.

name: Boquerón
description: A horizontal tricolor of blue, White and green.

name: Caaguazú
description: A horizontal tricolor of red, White and blue with 5 green stars in the center.

name: Caazapá
description: A horizontal bicolor of green and red with a white square ended on the upper hoist-side corner of the green band.

name: Canindeyú
description: a green with 3 bands of blue, White and red downwards.

name: Central
description: a White field with a red stripe and 19 blue stars.

name: Concepción
description: a diagonal bicolor of blue and White and a red stripe between.

name: Cordillera
description: Two equal horizontal bands of white (top) and green with a red-edged blue isosceles triangle based on the hoist side, a golden 12-pointed star inside the triangle and the department's arms in the center.[33]

name: Guairá
description: a horizontal bicolor of cyan and purple.

name: Itapúa
description: a horizontal tricolor of green, White and red with the department's arms in the center.

name: Misiones
description: a horizontal tricolor of yellow, White and green separated by two narrow stripes of red and blue and the department's arms in the center.

name: Ñeembucú
description: a horizontal tricolor of White, mint and blue.

name: Paraguarí
description: A white cross with the department's arms in the center that divides the flag into four rectangles, red and green at the top and green and red at the bottom.

name: Presidente Hayes
description: a horizontal tricolor of green (upwards), White and green.

name: San Pedro
description: a vertical tricolor of blue, White and green with the department's arms in the center.

name: Uruguay
description: White with blue stripes and sun

name: Venezuela
description: Yellow, blue and red

name: Amazonas
description: Horizontal tricolor of blue, green and red with a brown outline of the state (bordered in yellow) in the center. The map is charged with seven stars spread unevenly inside the map, showing the location of the state's seven municipalities, alongside an outline of Cerro Autana and a Yanomami's face. Ratio 2:3.

name: Anzoátegui
description: Main article: Flag of AnzoáteguiHorizontal tricolor of light blue, yellow and green with a black border outlining the state in the center and the state's coat of arms on the hoist side of the blue band. Ratio 2:3.

name: Apure
description: Horizontal triband of yellow, blue and green; with a white triangle based at the hoist side charged with the state's coat of arms and seven white stars on the blue band. Ratio 2:3.

name: Aragua
description: Bicolor diagonal quartered of red and yellow with the state's coat of arms in the center. Ratio 2:3.

name: Barinas
description: Horizontal tricolor of light blue, white and green, with a red square in the center of the white band, charged with a yellow rising sun, a road of the same color and a green palm tree. Ratio 2:3.

name: Bolívar
description: Main article: Flag of Bolívar StateYellow field with a green circle in the center, superposed by three horizontal blue stripes. The state's coat of arms is on the upper hoist side of the yellow field and the central blue stripe is charged with eight white stars. Ratio 110:168.

name: Capital District (Caracas)
description: Three triangles of red, blue and red, with a white star in the center. The base of the blue triangle contains in green the shape of Waraira Repano (Cerro El Ávila). Ratio 2:3.

name: Carabobo
description: Horizontal triband of red, blue and red again, in which the upper red band takes three fifths of the flag. The red band is charged with a yellow sun based on the fly side of the blue band. The sun is charged with the Arc of Carabobo. The upper side of the blue band is bordered by a green line.  Ratio 2:3.

name: Cojedes
description: Horizontal triband of orange, black and blue in which the orange band takes four sixths of the flag; with a blue circle charged with a yellow sun on the hoist side of the orange band. Ratio 2:3.

name: Delta Amacuro
description: Horizontal triband of light blue, green and blue; with a white, yellow, brown and black-edged blue triangle based on the hoist, charged with a green outline of the state. The proportion of the bands is 3:2:3; and the sky blue band is charged with an arc of four white stars.  Ratio 2:3.

name: Falcón
description: Blue field with a yellow rising sun on the upper hoist side and a white moon on the lower fly side; charged with a red chief bearing the words "Muera la Tiranía Viva la Libertad" ("Death to Tyranny and long live Freedom"). Based on Francisco de Miranda's naval flag. Ratio 2:3.

name: Federal Dependencies (Probably unofficial)
description: Horizontal tricolor of green, white and blue, with a red fish in the center of the white band. Ratio 2:3.

name: Guárico
description: Four horizontal bands of blue, white, yellow and green, with the state's coat of arms on the hoist side of the blue band; charged with a half-blue, half-green outline of the map bearing a cow's head sided by an ear of rice and an ear of sorghum, and an image of the Morros de San Juan. The map is surrounded by fourteen golden stars (seven on each side). Ratio 2:3.

name: Lara
description: Two horizontal bands of red and green, the red twice the size of the green. The red band is charged with a setting sun with thirteen yellow-edged white rays based on the green band. Ratio 2:3.

name: Mérida
description: Three triangles of green, white and light blue, with a red star in the center. The white central triangle is based on the bottom of the flag, dividing the green and light blue triangles. Ratio 2:3.

name: Miranda
description: Horizontal tricolor of black, red and yellow, with an arc of six white stars in the center of the red band and a yellow sun on the hoist side of the black band, charged with two cocoa branches and the words "Libertad o Muerte" ("Liberty or Death"). Based on Francisco de Miranda's military flag.  Ratio 2:3.

name: Monagas
description: Light blue field superposed by three horizontal bands of blue, green and black, with a yellow sun based on the middle of the green band. The light blue field is charged with a stylized black outline of Juana Ramírez under an arc of thirteen white stars Ratio 2:3.

name: Nueva Esparta
description: Horizontal tricolor of yellow, green and blue, in which the yellow band takes half of the flag; the green band is charged with three white stars and the yellow band is charged with a white semi-circle based on the fly side of the green band. Ratio 2:3.

name: Portuguesa
description: Two horizontal bands of blue and green separated by a narrow white stripe; the blue band is slightly larger than the green and has a white-edged sun on its upper hoist side. Ratio 2:3.

name: Sucre
description: Diagonal bisection of white and sky blue, with the state's coat of arms on the upper hoist side and fifteen white stars on the lower fly side. Ratio 2:3.

name: Táchira
description: Horizontal tricolor of yellow, black and red, with two coffee branches under an arc of four white stars. Ratio 2:3.

name: Trujillo
description: Two horizontal bands of red and white with a green triangle based on the hoist, charged with a white star bearing a pigeon. Ratio 2:3.

name: La Guaira (formerly known as Vargas)
description: Two horizontal bands of white and blue with proportions of 3:1 on the hoist side; the white field charged with a red-edged yellow sun and the blue with four white stars. To the fly side, four vertical bands of yellow, red, white and blue. Based on the flag of José María España and Manuel Gual. Ratio 2:3.

name: Yaracuy
description: A white diagonal band radiating from the lower hoist-side corner. The upper triangle is red, and the lower triangle is blue. On the center of the flag, a yellow sun with a circle showing a green field with brown mountains in the horizon, and a light blue sky with three white clouds. Ratio 2:3.

name: Zulia
description: Two horizontal bands of blue and black, with a yellow sun in the center charged with a white thunder bolt. Ratio 2:3.

name: Peru
description: Red, white and red

name: Suriname
description: Green, white, red

name: Australia
description: Blue with Union Jack and stars

name: New South Wales
description: A St George's Cross with four gold stars and a lion in the fly of a British blue ensign.[2]

name: Queensland
description: A light blue Maltese cross with a crown on a white background in the fly of a British blue ensign.[3]

name: South Australia
description: A piping shrike on a gold background in the fly of a British blue ensign.[4]

name: Tasmania
description: A red lion on a white background in the fly of a British blue ensign.[5]

name: Victoria
description: The Southern Cross surmounted by a crown in the fly of a British blue ensign.[6]

name: Western Australia
description: A black swan on a gold background in the fly of a British blue ensign.[7]

name: the Australian Capital Territory
description: A vertical 1:2 bicolour of blue (charged with the Southern Cross) and gold (charged with a modified Coat of arms of the city of Canberra)[8]

name: the Northern Territory
description: A vertical 1:2 bicolour of black (charged with the Southern Cross) and ochre (charged with a stylised Sturt's Desert Rose)[9]

name: Christmas Island
description: Blue and green diagonal bicolour charged with a southern cross of four seven-pointed white stars and one smaller five-pointed white star on the blue half, a golden bosun bird in gold on the green half, and a gold circle in the centre containing the shape of the island in green[10]

name: the Cocos (Keeling) Islands
description: A green field with a palm tree on a gold disc in the canton, a gold crescent in the centre of the flag and a gold southern cross in the fly.

name: Norfolk Island
description: Norfolk Island pine (Araucaria heterophylla) in a central white stripe between two green stripes[11]

name: New Zealand
description: Blue with Union Jack and stars

name: Nelson
description: The flag is based on the City of Nelson's coat of arms.[12]

name: Otago
description: Per fess dancetty azure and or, two mullets of eight points in pale counterchanged.

name: Tokelau
description: A yellow Tokelauan canoe sailing towards the Southern Cross represented by four white stars on a blue field

name: the Cook Islands
description: A British Blue Ensign, with a ring made of fifteen stars defaced on the fly.

name: Niue
description: A golden yellow flag with the Union flag in the upper left (hoist) quarter of the flags. On the Union Jack are two yellow five-pointed stars on the vertical stripe and two on the horizontal stripe. In the centre of the Union flag is a blue disc with another, slightly larger, yellow five-pointed star.

name: French collectivities in Melanesia
description: Blue, white and red vertical

name: Flags of New Caledonia
description:
A vertical tricolour of blue, white, and red.
A horizontal tricolour of blue, red, and green charged with a yellow disc outlined in black and defaced with a black flèche faîtière.

name: Indonesia
description: Red and white horizontal

name: Papua New Guinea
description: Red and black with bird

name: the Autonomous Region of Bougainville
description: Red and white upe headdress superimposed on a green and white kapkap, on a field of cobalt blue.

name: Solomon Islands
description: Blue and green with stars

name: Vanuatu
description: Red, green and yellow

name: the Malampa Province
description: A descending diagonal tricolour of green-yellow-blue, with three black stars and a native canoe in the centre.

name: the Penama Province
description: Divided horizontally red-light blue-black-light blue-green (5:2:2:2:5). This flag had a white canton, with a boar's tusk, surrounded by a wreath of leaves of the namele fern. In the upper red stripe is a yellow five-pointed star, in the lower green stripe are two yellow five-pointed stars.[15]

name: the Sanma Province
description: Light blue, with the national flag in the canon. The fly bears twelve white stars in four rows of three, and the lower fly carries two yellow cogwheels, one inside the other.[16]

name: the Shefa Province
description: A horizontal tricolour of blue-red-green, with the national flag (approximately one-sixth size along the hoist) in the canton. The fly a white-rimmed golden disk, extending into the blue and green stripes.[17]

name: the Tafea Province
description: A light blue, a central horizontal stripe of yellow-red-yellow, five white stars, and a green canton, with a yellow cross and black shield.

name: the Torba Province
description: Diagonally arranged, blue in the upper hoist, stripes of red-yellow-black-white-green, and yellow in the lower fly. with the emblem in the lower yellow triangular field.[18]

name: Federated States of Micronesia
description: Blue with four white stars

name: Pohnpei
description: A flag featuring a wreath of coconut leaves with six stars and a sakau cup in the middle, on a blue field.

name: United States in Micronesia
description: Stars and stripes

name: Guam
description: A dark blue background with a thin red border and the Seal in the center.

name: the Northern Mariana Islands
description: A circle of flowers and plants with a gray sculpture and a white star on a blue field.

name: Wake Island
description: Unofficial flag.

name: the Federated States of Micronesia
description: A light blue field with four white five-pointed stars arranged in the diamond pattern in the center.

name: the Marshall Islands
description: A blue field with two diagonal stripes of orange and white radiating from the lower hoist-side corner to the upper fly-side corner and the large white star with four large rays and twenty small rays on the upper hoist-side corner above the stripes.

name: Palau
description: A light blue field with the large yellow disk shifted slightly to the hoist-side of center.

name: French collectivities in Polynesia
description: Red and white with emblem

name: French Polynesia
description: Two red horizontal bands encase a wide white band in a 1:2:1 ratio, with the Coat of arms centred on the white stripe

name: French Polynesia
description: Red and white with emblem

name: the Austral Islands
description: The flag has a ratio of 2:3 and is divided into three vertical stripes: red, white and red, with the proportions 1:3:1. In the white central stripe is a blue emblem surrounded by five stars.

name: the Gambier Islands
description: Three horizontal stripes of equal width in the order white-blue-white charged with a blue star in each corner and a white star in the middle.

name: the Marquesas Islands
description: A flag divided in to a top yellow half and a bottom red half with a white triangle in the hoist charged with the design of Maitiki.

name: United Kingdom in Polynesia
description: Red, white and blue with crosses

name: Pitcairn Islands
description: A Blue Ensign charged in the fly with the coat of arms of the Pitcairn Islands

name: United States in Polynesia
description: Stars and stripes

name: Hawaii
description: Eight alternating horizontal stripes of white, red, and blue, with the United Kingdom's Union Flag (ratio 4:7) in the canton

name: American Samoa
description: A red-edged white triangle pointing towards the hoist charged with a bald eagle clutching a war club and a fly-whisk. The white triangle divides the dark blue field into two separate triangles.

name: Wallis and Futuna
description: Red and blue

name: Fiji
description: Light blue with coat of arms

name: Kiribati
description: Red and blue with rising sun

name: Marshall Islands
description: Blue with orange and white

name: Nauru
description: Blue with yellow stripe

name: Palau
description: Blue with yellow circle

name: Samoa
description: Red and blue with white stars

name: Tonga
description: Red and white with cross

name: Tuvalu
description: Light blue with Union Jack
